title: Succession

characters.name: Logan Roy
characters.archetype: The patriarch. Founder. Refuses to die. The whole show orbits him. Says 'fuck off' more than any human in TV history.

characters.name: Kendall Roy
characters.archetype: The chosen heir who was never quite chosen. Wants it desperately. Falls apart in public. The L to the OG rap.

characters.name: Shiv Roy
characters.archetype: The only daughter. Smartest. Quietly the most ruthless. Keeps getting passed over. Eventually betrayed by Tom AND her brothers.

characters.name: Roman Roy
characters.archetype: The youngest, the funniest, the most damaged. Says the cruelest things while crying. Failed eulogy at Logan's funeral.

characters.name: Connor Roy
characters.archetype: The eldest. Half-brother. Thinks he's running for President. Mostly forgotten. Marries Willa.

characters.name: Tom Wambsgans
characters.archetype: Shiv's husband. Operator. Survivor. Boar on the floor. Eventually wins everything.

characters.name: Greg Hirsch
characters.archetype: The cousin nobody wanted. Falls upward through pure incompetence and proximity. Married 'Tom-and-Greg' to him forever.

characters.name: Gerri Kellman
characters.archetype: Waystar's general counsel. Roman's complicated almost-something. The actual adult in the room.

characters.name: Stewy Hosseini
characters.archetype: Kendall's college bestie turned hostile activist investor. The 'Bear Hug' enabler.

characters.name: Frank Vernon
characters.archetype: The old guard. Logan's sometime confidante. Always one of the oldest people in any room.

characters.name: Karl Muller
characters.archetype: Smug CFO. The 'real money' alpha. Plays both sides every season.

characters.name: Marcia Roy
characters.archetype: Logan's third wife. Quiet menace. Disappears mid-show, returns for the funeral.

characters.name: Lukas Matsson
characters.archetype: Swedish tech bro. The buyer of Waystar. Tom's eventual boss.

characters.name: Willa Ferreyra
characters.archetype: Connor's escort-turned-wife. Reluctant playwright. The 'is this all there is' face.

characters.name: Naomi Pierce
characters.archetype: Old-money Pierce family heir. Kendall's on-and-off girlfriend.

signature_quotes: I love you, but you are not serious people.
You can't make a Tomlette without breaking some Greggs.
I don't even want to be CEO of Pierce, I want to be CEO of Sweden!
What is a person but their context?
I might bury you, but I love you.
She loves me. We were meant to be together.
If it is to be said, so it be, so it is.
viral_moments: Logan dying on the plane in S4E3 — quietest TV moment of the year.
Kendall's '90s rap performance for Logan's birthday.
Boar on the floor — the dinner of psychological torture.
Roman's failed eulogy crying at Logan's funeral.
The election night episode — the kids decide an election.
The 'Tom and Greg do crimes' season finale.
Shiv at the boardroom vote, waving as Tom becomes CEO.
Connor's wedding to Willa (the saddest party in TV history).
memes: 'Tomlette' as universal shorthand for any business cover-up.
Boar on the floor (the corporate humiliation ritual) used in every workplace meme.
'L to the OG' performed at hundreds of weddings since.
'I love you but you are not serious people' as the universal Twitter dunk.
Greg getting taller / falling upward — the cousin Greg energy.
'I'd be incredibly good at being CEO' as the white-male overconfidence meme.
Roman's lap-dance scene with Gerri (don't look it up).
'We here. We together.' as the awkward-emotional-support phrase.
off_screen_drama: Jeremy Strong's method acting documented in a New Yorker profile became its own scandal — co-stars defended/rolled their eyes.
Brian Cox is famously not impressed with method acting; called it 'pretentious'.
Kieran Culkin won the Emmy for S4 — the speech where he asked Jesse Eisenberg for kids was iconic.
Sarah Snook gave birth between S3 and S4; her pregnancy was hidden in S4.
Jesse Armstrong ended the show after S4 to avoid 'overstaying its welcome'.
The cast did press together post-finale wearing matching pins — fans loved it.
Multiple cast members (Snook, Macfadyen, Strong) won major awards within 6 months of finale.
running_jokes: Roman dictates corporate strategy from a lap of his couch.
Tom's collection of grievances about being treated badly by Shiv.
Logan's kids never doing the actual work but inheriting the rewards.
Karl always being two steps ahead of everyone.
Shiv's fashion getting sharper as her professional life falls apart.
